name: Vada Pav
description: The 'Indian Burger' – a spicy potato fritter served in a soft bun with dry garlic chutney.

steps: Sauté mashed potatoes with spices and green chili paste.
Dip potato balls in gram flour batter and deep fry.
Serve in pav with spicy red garlic chutney.

name: Misal Pav
description: A fiery sprouts curry topped with crunchy farsan and served with soft bread.

steps: Cook sprouts and prepare a spicy thin gravy (Kat).
Layer sprouts, farsan, and onions in a bowl.
Pour the hot gravy over and serve with Pav.

name: Pithla Bhakri
description: A rustic, thick gram-flour curry served with handmade sorghum (Jowar) bread.

steps: Make a gram flour slurry and sauté with garlic and chilies.
Cook until thick and steamy.
Serve with hot, handmade Bhakri.

name: Puran Poli
description: A sweet, buttery flatbread stuffed with a paste of chana dal and jaggery.

steps: Cook dal with jaggery and mash into a smooth paste (Puran).
Stuff inside wheat dough and roll thin.
Roast with ghee until golden spots appear.

name: Solkadhi
description: A refreshing pink digestive drink made from coconut milk and kokum fruit.

steps: Mix fresh coconut milk with kokum extract.
Add crushed garlic and chili for a spicy kick.
Chill and serve as a digestive.

name: Bharli Vangi
description: Small brinjals stuffed with a nutty, spicy masala of peanuts and coconut.

steps: Stuff slit brinjals with roasted peanut-coconut masala.
Slow-cook in a pan until the brinjals are tender.
Serve with rice or Bhakri.

name: Ukadiche Modak
description: Steamed rice flour dumplings filled with fresh coconut and jaggery.

steps: Cook coconut and jaggery for the sweet filling.
Prepare steamed rice flour dough and shape into petals.
Stuff, seal, and steam until glossy.

name: Bombil Fry
description: Crispy fried Bombay Duck fish coated in semolina (rava).

steps: Flatten fish to remove moisture and marinate with spices.
Coat with semolina and rice flour.
Fry until golden and extremely crispy.

name: Kolhapuri Rassa
description: A duo of mutton broths: Tambada (Spicy Red) and Pandhara (White Coconut-based).

steps: Pressure cook mutton to get the stock.
For Tambada: Sauté Masala and add stock for a thin fiery gravy.
For Pandhara: Mix stock with coconut milk and mild spices.

name: Kanda Poha
description: Flattened rice sautéed with onions, turmeric, and crunchy peanuts.

steps: Wash and drain poha. Keep aside.
Temper mustard seeds, onions, and peanuts until golden.
Mix poha with turmeric, salt, and lemon. Steam for 2 mins.

name: Sabudana Khichdi
description: A chewy, savory mix of soaked tapioca pearls and roasted peanuts.

steps: Soak sabudana overnight until fluffy.
Mix with crushed roasted peanuts.
Sauté cumin, chilies, and potatoes; add sabudana and cook until translucent.

name: Thalipeeth
description: A multi-grain pancake made with 'Bhajani' flour, onions, and cilantro.

steps: Knead a dough of Bhajani flour with onions and spices.
Flatten by hand on a wet cloth into a circle.
Roast on a griddle with holes for oil until crispy.

name: Shrikhand
description: A thick, creamy hung-yogurt dessert flavored with saffron and cardamom.

steps: Hang yogurt in a muslin cloth to remove all whey (Chakka).
Mix Chakka with sugar until smooth.
Fold in saffron milk and cardamom powder.

name: Kharda/Thecha
description: A fiery, coarse paste made from roasted green chilies and garlic.

steps: Roast chilies and garlic in oil until blistered.
Crush coarsely using a mortar and pestle with salt.
Serve with hot Bhakri.

name: Maharashtrian Amti
description: A sweet, tangy, and spicy pigeon pea dal flavored with Goda Masala.

steps: Cook tur dal until mushy.
Add Goda Masala, tamarind pulp, and jaggery.
Temper with mustard seeds, asafoetida, and curry leaves.

name: Saoji Chicken
description: A legendary, ultra-spicy curry from the Halba Koshti community, known for its unique blend of 32 spices.

steps: Dry roast the Saoji masala spices until dark and aromatic.
Sauté onions and coconut paste until oil separates.
Slow-cook the chicken in the spice base until it develops a deep, dark 'Tari' (oil layer).

name: Vangi Bharit
description: Smoky mashed eggplant prepared with spring onions and peanuts, specifically famous during the 'Farit' festivals.

steps: Roast the eggplant over an open flame until the skin is charred.
Peel and mash the smoky pulp.
Sauté with plenty of spring onions, peanuts, and green chili paste. Do not overcook.

name: Kothimbir Vadi
description: Savory cilantro and gram flour cakes that are steamed and then fried to crispy perfection.

steps: Mix a thick batter of chopped coriander, gram flour, and spices.
Steam the batter as a block until firm.
Cut into squares and deep-fry until the exterior is crunchy.

name: Bhajani Chakli
description: A crunchy, spiral-shaped savory snack made from a special multi-grain flour called 'Bhajani'.

steps: Knead the Bhajani flour with hot water and butter for a soft dough.
Use a 'Chakli Press' to create spiral shapes.
Deep fry on a medium flame until they stop bubbling and become crisp.

name: Shev Bhaji
description: A spicy, watery gravy (rassa) served with thick, spicy sev (fried gram flour noodles).

steps: Prepare a thin, spicy red gravy using Kala Masala and roasted onion paste.
Bring the gravy to a boil.
Add the thick shev just before serving to keep it from getting too soggy.

name: Basundi
description: Sweetened condensed milk flavored with nutmeg, cardamom, and a bounty of dry fruits.

steps: Boil milk in a thick-bottomed pan, reducing it to half its volume.
Scrape the cream from the sides and mix it back in.
Add sugar and spices, and garnish with roasted nuts and saffron.
